mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods with rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold signal shows Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >58% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below baseline during periods of payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with elevated federal funds rates signals a synchronized downturn in interest-sensitive demand sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable unemployment rates reflects supply-side resilience without labor market stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in <50% of cases during recovery phases, or the interaction term adds no predictive value over individual series in walk-forward validation.
